pr.title: [AI Agent Test] Decompile 10 stub functions across ft, gm, gr, it modules
pr.author: stephenjayakar

summary: Merged experimental AI-agent decompilation PR that replaced stub comments with matching C for 8 listed functions across fighter, game-mode, stage, and item code, and removed two already-matching stub markers. The work also refined several signatures and local structs/headers so small accessor and state-reset functions could be expressed through typed fields rather than M2C_FIELD or UNK_RET placeholders. The author explicitly noted this was an agent scaffold test, that the title overcounted because there were 8 matches, and that they were concerned about changed function signatures; there was no review-comment evidence in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented fn_800DC014 in ftCo_Attack100.c: reads GET_FIGHTER(gobj), compares capturewait.x0 against p_ftCommonData->x3AC, and sets capturewait.xC when HSD_PAD_XY input is held.
Added a capturewait motion-vars member to union ftCommon_MotionVars with x0 float, padding to offset 0xC, and xC u8 so fn_800DC014 could use structured field access.
Replaced M2C_FIELD-based returns in gm_80169520, gm_80169530, and gm_80169540 with typed lbl_8046B488 fields x20, xA2, and x124.
Implemented fn_80169550 in gm_1601.c as a void function that indexes lbl_8046B488.x1A6 by slot, then writes -1 to lbl_8046B488.x20[idx].
Expanded struct lbl_8046B488_t in gm_1601.static.h to split a large pad into typed s8 arrays at offsets 0x20, 0xA2, 0x124, and 0x1A6.
Implemented gm_8017E5C8 as an Adventure table accessor returning lbl_803D7AC0[stage_slot + difficulty * 5].pad_6[arg2 * 3].
Implemented gm_80182554 as a state initializer/resetter for lbl_80472ED8, assigning x6C8 and x6C4 from arguments and zeroing x6BC, x6C0, and x6BE.
Added a typed lbl_80472ED8 object in gm_17C0.c with explicit fields at bss-relative offsets x6BC, x6BE, x6C0, x6C4, and x6C8.
Adjusted several gm_17C0 Adventure/All-Star helper signatures and uses from int difficulty/count to u8 where the decompiled code and function-pointer types required byte-sized values.
Updated UnkAdventureData function pointer types in gm/types.h from int-based difficulty parameters to u8-based parameters for x4C, x64, and x68.
Implemented grCorneria_801E2598 as a boolean-style accessor over extern grCn_803E2204[arg0][arg1].data[0], with a local grCn_Entry typedef.
Implemented grOldPupupu_802109D8 to zero Ground gv.unk fields xC4, xC8, xCC, set xD0 to 1, and call grAnime_801C8138 with gobj, gp->map_id, and 0.
Removed stale stub comments for grIceMt_801F8364 and grOldKongo_8020F4E8, which the PR body says were already matched.
Expanded itPikachuthunder_ItemVars in itCharItems.h from mostly padding to named offset fields x0 through x38, and added itPikachuthunderAttributes with three f32 fields.
Implemented it_802B1FE8 to compute a position from item position plus a scaled negative thunder attribute offset on y.
Implemented it_802F1344 for Crazy Hand bomb: gets Item, uses PAD_STACK(8), calls Item_80268E5C(gobj, 1, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE), and assigns ip->on_accessory = it_802F13B0.
Changed itCrazyHandBomb_Logic86_DmgDealt to call it_802F1344(gobj), and updated it_802F1344/it_802F13B0 prototypes to typed Item_GObj*/HSD_GObj* signatures.
smart_moves: Used struct-field recovery to eliminate raw M2C_FIELD expressions in gm_1601, making array access and the fn_80169550 write clearer and more reusable.
Matched small state routines by identifying that they only reset or initialize known offsets, avoiding overcomplicated control flow.
Propagated signature changes into headers and call sites instead of only changing C definitions, including gm_17C0.h, gm_1601.h, gm/types.h, gmadventure.c, and itcrazyhandbomb.h.
Preserved offset evidence in newly introduced structs with offset comments and padding, especially in ftCommon_MotionVars, lbl_8046B488_t, and itPikachuthunder_ItemVars.

For Adventure table helpers, normalized repeated index math to stage_slot + difficulty * 5 once the difficulty parameter was typed as u8, which also removed explicit casts that had been compensating for incorrect signatures.
Recognized and removed already-matched stub comments for stage functions rather than forcing unnecessary changes.
decomp_lessons: Small stubs often become straightforward once neighboring accessors reveal the correct global struct layout; recover the shared struct first, then implement the stub using typed fields.

If assembly requires byte-sized arithmetic or return values, changing parameters and return types to u8 may be part of the match, but this must be propagated through headers, function-pointer structs, and callers.
For motion vars unions, add the specific action-state struct member with exact offsets instead of reusing unrelated union members or raw padding.
When an item callback assignment does not match, check the callback's exact HSD_GObj*/Item_GObj* signature and update declarations consistently.
Removing a `/// #symbol` stub marker is appropriate when the function body was already matching; not every PR line needs a new function body.
Local extern table typedefs can unblock stage accessors, but they should remain conservative when the full data layout is not known.
naming_conventions: Unknown recovered fields keep offset-derived names such as x20, xA2, x124, x1A6, x6BC, x6BE, x6C0, x6C4, and x6C8.
New item var and attribute structs follow existing item naming style: itPikachuthunder_ItemVars and itPikachuthunderAttributes.
Function names stayed address-based or module-prefixed where semantic names were not known, such as fn_800DC014, fn_80169550, gm_8017E5C8, gm_80182554, it_802B1FE8, and it_802F1344.
Offset comments were retained or added in struct definitions, including item-var comments that show both struct-relative and Item offset information such as `+0 +DD4`.
Boolean-like stage accessors may still return s32 when the surrounding signature or matching context expects it, as with grCorneria_801E2598 returning `val != 0`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit u8 parameter and local types to influence PowerPC codegen for table-indexing helpers instead of relying on repeated `(u8)` casts.
For float percentage helpers, writing `field / 100.0F` after the field type is known can produce the needed conversion without explicit `(f32)` casts.
PAD_STACK(8) was required in it_802F1344, indicating stack-shape preservation was necessary for the match.
Representing lbl_8046B488 subregions as s8 arrays enabled direct indexed stores such as `lbl_8046B488.x20[idx] = -1`.
Implementing gm_80182554 as direct stores to a partially typed bss object matched a simple zero/reset sequence.
The ftCo capture-wait match used nested conditionals rather than a combined boolean expression, preserving likely branch structure.
review_feedback: No formal reviews or review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body self-reported that the title was inaccurate: it said 10 stub functions, but the author noted there were 8 matches.
The author described the PR as mostly an experiment to try an agent scaffold.
The author expressed concern that the agent changed other function signatures; the diff confirms several signature updates in gm_17C0, gm_1601, gm/types.h, gmadventure.c, and itcrazyhandbomb.h.
The author said the work used skills from PR #2076, but this slice does not include enough detail to identify those skills beyond the observable struct/signature cleanup.
follow_up_queries: Find PR #2076 to understand the referenced matching skills and whether they apply to agent-generated stub decompilation.
Audit the gm_17C0 signature changes from int to u8 against all function-pointer call paths to ensure no ABI mismatch was introduced.
Check whether local grCn_Entry and extern grCn_803E2204 should move to a shared stage data header once more Corneria table layout is known.
Validate itPikachuthunder_ItemVars and itPikachuthunderAttributes names/field types against other Pikachu thunder functions before assigning semantic field names.
Search for remaining M2C_FIELD uses around lbl_8046B488 that can reuse the recovered x20/xA2/x124/x1A6 arrays.
